headline: OpenAI head of robotics resigns after deal with Pentagon
summary: Caitlin Kalinowski, head of robotics and consumer hardware at OpenAI, announced her resignation ‌on Saturday, citing concerns about the company's agreement with ‌the Department of Defense.  In a social media post on X, Kalinowski wrote that ​OpenAI did not take enough time before agreeing to deploy its AI models on the Pentagon's classified cloud networks.  "AI has an important role in national security," Kalinowski posted.

"AI has an important role in national security," Kalinowski posted.

headline: AMD Meta AI Deal Puts Valuation Gap And Growth Story In Focus
summary: AMD (NasdaqGS:AMD) announced a multi year, gigawatt scale AI infrastructure partnership with Meta Platforms, valued at more than $100b. The agreement includes custom Instinct GPUs, performance warrants, and broad infrastructure collaboration across Meta's AI data centers. AMD also introduced Ryzen AI 400 Series chips for AI focused PCs and expanded research and telecom alliances in Canada, Vietnam, and with Wind River. headline: Oracle Resets AI Data Center Push As Layoffs Test Growth Story
summary: Oracle plans to cut thousands of jobs across multiple divisions as it works to manage costs tied to its AI data center buildout. The company is reportedly reallocating spending toward large cloud infrastructure projects for clients such as OpenAI and Meta. Oracle and OpenAI have also decided to cancel a planned flagship AI data center project in Texas. For investors watching NYSE:ORCL, these moves come with the stock trading around $152.96, after a 5.2% gain over the past week and 4.3% over...
